title: rubus macrophyllus subsp. schlechtendalii
intro: Rubus macrophyllus subsp. schlechtendalii, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or giant Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the Himalayan raspberry plant, a type of bramble native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus macrophyllus subsp. schlechtendalii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, juice, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and fever.
